title: Polymorphic printing via virtual print
prompt_md:
## Goal

Make `std::cout << obj` print the right thing for both a base `Shape` and a derived `Circle`, even when the `Circle` is reached through a `const Shape&`. The stream operator itself cannot be virtual, so route it through a virtual member function that each class supplies, and provide that operator exactly once.

## Input

- Line 1: a `name` (single word, 1-50 characters).
- Line 2: a `radius` (a double).

## Output

Exactly three lines:
1. a `Shape` named `name`, printed directly: `[Shape: <name>]`
2. a `Circle` (same name, given radius), printed directly: `[Circle: <name>, r=<radius>]`
3. the *same* `Circle`, printed through a `const Shape&`: `[Circle: <name>, r=<radius>]`

`<radius>` uses default `std::cout` formatting (no fixed precision). Line 3 must come out identical to line 2 -- that is what proves the dispatch is polymorphic and not based on the static reference type.

title: Polymorphic employee directory
prompt_md:
## Goal

Build an `Employee` hierarchy whose objects can all be streamed with `std::cout << emp` and produce type-correct output -- without writing a separate `operator<<` for each class. Two concrete roles exist: `Manager` (carries a department) and `Engineer` (carries a primary language). Store a mix of them as `Employee*` and stream every one through the single base streaming operator.

## Input

- Line 1: an integer `N` (1 <= N <= 10).
- Next `N` lines: either `manager <name> <dept>` or `engineer <name> <lang>` (all single words, no spaces).

## Output

For each employee, in input order, one line:
- a manager: `Manager: <name>, dept=<dept>`
- an engineer: `Engineer: <name>, lang=<lang>`

Then a final line: `Total: <N>`.

test_cases.label: Mixed manager and engineer
test_cases.stdin:
3
manager Alice Sales
engineer Bob C++
manager Carol HR
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Manager: Alice, dept=Sales
Engineer: Bob, lang=C++
Manager: Carol, dept=HR
Total: 3

test_cases.label: All engineers
test_cases.stdin:
2
engineer Dave Python
engineer Eve Rust
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Engineer: Dave, lang=Python
Engineer: Eve, lang=Rust
Total: 2

test_cases.label: Longer interleaved directory
test_cases.stdin:
4
engineer Grace Go
manager Heidi Finance
manager Ivan Legal
engineer Judy Java
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Engineer: Grace, lang=Go
Manager: Heidi, dept=Finance
Manager: Ivan, dept=Legal
Engineer: Judy, lang=Java
Total: 4
